title: v0.5 orientation — engagement management workstream kickoff, PI-001 reopening, paper-test deferral
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: 'Continued work on V2' against the project default ARCHITECTURE operating mode.

Discovery walk through repository state. Read CLAUDE.md (684 lines, root), the v2 db-export JSON snapshots (status.json v13 'v0.4 complete', sessions.json with 24 entries, decisions.json with 74 entries, planning_items.json with 16 entries, plus references, charter, change_log), the four MVS methodology entity schema specs at methodology-schema-specs/, ui-PRD-v0.4.md, the workstream plans, the conduct framework at PRDs/process/conduct/, the evolved-methodology phase outline, the catalog ingestion artifacts, and the recent paper-test kickoff at methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md authored earlier same day.

State reconciliation. User memory was last updated around SES-011 (05-11-26) and was 5 days stale relative to actual state. Between SES-011 and conversation open: schema-design conversations SES-012–015 (domain, entity, process, crm_candidate), catalog ingestion SES-016 (42-entry base entity catalog into v2 database), v0.4-build-planning SES-017, v0.4 PRD reconciliation SES-018, v0.4 slices A–F as SES-019–024, conduct framework integration commits 2f47c20 + a5cb163, and the paper-test kickoff commit 75f2432. Status payload now reads 'phase: v0.4 complete'.

Surfacing of the actual confusion. Six candidate sources of confusion catalogued: memory drift, the unresolved coexistence of the current 13-phase Document Production Process and the evolved 5-phase methodology, three different meanings of 'entity' (methodology entity type / the methodology entity type literally named 'entity' / catalog entity), v1 and v2 not yet connected for the deployment path, PI-001 four-times-deferred styling, and the paper-test authored-but-unrun status. User pointed at the data-co-mingling problem: dogfooded v2.db is reserved for v2-build governance; testing CBM there would mix two unrelated bodies of data.

The DEC-039 multi-tenancy posture (one v2 instance per engagement; separate SQLite, separate API port; finding-not-decision) was rediscovered together with the operational mechanism gap (the code supports env-var-pointed alternate instances via CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH, CRMBUILDER_V2_API_PORT, CRMBUILDER_V2_API_BASE_URL, but no second instance has been created, no naming convention exists, and no runbook exists). The 'no second instance' state is exactly what would have been hit mid-Phase-1 if the paper-test had opened next.

Discovery of v1's existing data model. Read automation/db/master_schema.py (Client master DB with id/name/code/project_folder/crm_platform/deployment_model fields), automation/db/client_schema.py (31-table per-client SQLite schema at {project_folder}/.crmbuilder/{code}.db with Domain/Entity/Field/FieldOption/Relationship/Persona/BusinessObject/Process/ProcessStep/Requirement/Decision/AISession/ChangeLog/Layout*/Instance/DeploymentRun tables), automation/importer/ (25 files, Path B parsers and mappers for master_prd/business_object_discovery/domain_overview/domain_reconciliation/entity_prd/process_definition/crm_selection/crm_deployment/yaml_generation). Side-by-side comparison surfaced that v1 already has nearly every methodology entity type v2's deferred PIs (PI-003 persona, PI-004 field/requirement/manual_config/test_spec, PI-005 process_step) catalog as future work. v2's stated 'database as source of truth' rationale (DEC-001) is the same architectural goal v1 has been incrementally pursuing for two years.

Reframing from the user. The v2 plan was governance-first then incremental migration of v1 functionality onto v2's foundation. Engagement management is just the next migration item — not a deviation from the plan. PI-003/004/005 are similarly migration items, not new inventions.

The consequential decision settled. Path A (build proper Engagement management in v2 as v0.5) vs Path B (Frankenstein bridge reading v1's master.db) was weighed via the eight-element template. Costs are roughly equivalent (both require a registry mechanism, active-engagement context, switcher UI, launcher, tests). Path A produces no bridge code requiring eventual unwinding; Path B does. Path A wins on the consistent-with-plan and no-technical-debt dimensions. Path C (env-var override only, no UI) was named for completeness but not seriously considered.

v0.5 workstream shape proposed and approved. Three conversations: this v0.5-orientation conversation (produces the workstream plans and the paper-test deferral), Conversation 1 (combined multi-engagement architecture + engagement schema design, because architecture and schema are tightly coupled — engagement record field shape depends on routing decisions), Conversation 2 (v0.5 build planning produces PRD + implementation plan + Claude Code slice prompts). Then build slices A–D and closeout.

Ten architectural questions catalogued for Conversation 1 to settle: discovery model (bootstrap DB vs directory scan), per-engagement DB file location, active-engagement state persistence, API server model (multi-tenant vs one-process-per-engagement), identifier scope (global vs per-engagement), migrations across engagements, the dogfood-migration mechanism, per-engagement exports location, MCP server routing, engagement entity field set.

PI-001 disposition resolved. The four-deferral pattern with progressively-elaborate trigger conditions that never fire was named as an anti-pattern. CBM-redo-friction trigger from DEC-042 cannot fire in v0.5's timeframe either (CBM redo waits on v0.5 + paper-test + Phase 1). Two options weighed: defer a fifth time, or reopen as parallel work alongside v0.5. Reopening chosen because the work is independent of v0.5's data/routing layer, scheduling certainty exceeds clean release scoping, and Phase 1 (post-v0.5) lands on a coherent system rather than a Qt-default one. Boundary discipline named: styling owns visual layer (QSS, design tokens, palette, typography, spacing, panel chrome); v0.5 owns data/routing layer (config, ActiveEngagementContext, alembic, API routing, engagement entity). One coupling point: v0.5's engagement panel inherits whatever tokens are current at slice land time; either order ships fine.

Paper-test disposition resolved. The paper-test kickoff at methodology-schemas-cbm-paper-test-kickoff.md predates v0.5 and assumes one v2 instance. Running it now would land CBM domain content in the v2-build dogfood instance. Deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement is created. A 'Status: Deferred' blockquote header was added to the kickoff document; body content unchanged.

Close-out path chosen. Path 1 (Claude Code prompt at conversation close, mirroring v0.4's slice G precedent at commit 9cbf02d) selected over Path 2 (typing each record into the desktop New Session dialog). Path 1 produces a reviewable artifact; Path 2 was named for completeness.

Plus the close-out payload PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_026.json (this payload) and the apply prompt PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-026.md. title: v0.5 Conversation 1 — multi-engagement architecture and engagement schema
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/v0.5-conversation-1-kickoff.md against the project default ARCHITECTURE operating mode.

Pre-flight. Read crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md, PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/v0.5-engagement-management-workstream-plan.md (the ten architectural questions agenda), methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md, methodology-schema-specs/domain.md end-to-end plus the §3 heading structure of entity.md, process.md, and crm_candidate.md (confirming template uniformity and noting process's no-status-field deviation and crm_candidate's removed-status deviation), the v0.4 PRD §2/§6/§7-9, the v2 runtime config.py (existing env-var override mechanism), v1's automation/db/master_schema.py (Client.code regex constraint and master-DB pattern precedent), v1's automation/ui/active_client_context.py (in-memory + signal + dual-persistence pattern), DEC-039 (the original multi-tenancy finding), and the SES-025 close-out payload (DEC-075/076/077). Verified SES-025 close-out had been applied: sessions=25, decisions=77, three decided_in refs to SES-025 present, PI-001 status still Open with description amended. git status clean; git pull --rebase clean.

Framing note before Q1. DEC-039 is the prior context being operationalised, not superseded. The weak priors in the kickoff all match DEC-039's framing exactly. Two precedents banked for later questions: v1's database_path column was deprecated in favor of convention (real-world supporting evidence for Q2's convention-based-paths posture); v1's active-state persistence is actually hybrid (Client.last_opened_at on master row plus last_selected_client_id in preferences), which informed Q3's framing.

Q1 — Engagement discovery model. Bootstrap meta DB vs directory scan. Option A approved: bootstrap meta DB at crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements.db with an engagements registry table. Separates routing metadata from methodology content; mirrors v1's master-DB pattern; supports operations on inactive engagements. Sub-question resolved later in the conversation: the engagement record IS the meta DB row (not a pointer to records inside per-engagement DBs).

Q2 — Per-engagement DB file location. Engine-repo conventional vs split vs convention-with-override. Option A approved: engine repo conventional paths at crmbuilder-v2/data/engagements/{engagement_code}.db, gitignored. No path field on engagement record. v1's split is a real precedent but adopting it now adds coupling to client-repo structure that v0.5's known use cases don't require.

Q7 — Dogfood migration. (Taken next because Q1 + Q2 settled enough foundation to make Q7's options concrete.) One-shot explicit migration vs permanent dual-path vs configurable path. Option A approved: one-shot explicit migration with backup-first, verify-row-counts, then delete-original discipline. Idempotent on rerun. Three install scenarios handled: existing v2 install (migrate), fresh install (no-op, meta DB empty, user creates engagements explicitly), rerun after successful migration (exit cleanly). The 24 sessions / 74 decisions / 16 PIs / 66 references / 222 change_log entries / 42 catalog entries / 13 status versions / 2 charter versions all survive.

Q3 — Active-engagement state persistence. Two distinct facts framed: live state (singleton at v2-install level) and ordering (per-engagement). Option A approved for live state: JSON file at crmbuilder-v2/data/current_engagement.json holding {engagement_identifier, engagement_code, set_at} plus in-memory ActiveEngagementContext QObject mirroring v1's pattern. Ordering: engagements.last_opened_at column on the meta DB. The hybrid mirrors v1's actual pattern.

Q4 — API server model. One-process-per-engagement vs multi-tenant vs hot-swap admin endpoint. Option A approved for v0.5 (one process per engagement, kill-and-relaunch on switch) with the user-added direction that Option B (multi-tenant) be committed as a follow-on migration. Migration timing clarified through a brief back-and-forth: not 'v0.6 specifically' or 'highest-priority v0.6+ candidate' but 'when v2 transitions from prototype to production.' This trigger is a planned transition rather than a friction signal (avoiding the PI-001 anti-pattern). PI-017 authored to track the migration work.

Q9 — MCP server routing. Forced by Q4. Decided-and-announced: MCP server lifecycle mirrors API — one MCP subprocess per engagement, killed and relaunched on switch. Same constraints, same migration path to multi-tenant at prototype-to-production transition.

Q5 — Identifier scope. Decided-and-announced: per-engagement scope for governance and methodology identifiers (SES, DEC, PI, RSK, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT, PROC, CRMC) — happens automatically because v0.4 next-identifier helpers query the active DB connection. Engagement identifiers (ENG) scope to the meta DB — one sequence per v2 install.

Q6 — Migrations across engagements. Lazy at engagement-open vs eager at v2 launch vs explicit only. Option A approved: lazy at engagement-open, mirroring v1's run_client_migrations pattern. Defers work to when needed; failure modes scoped to one engagement at a time; never-opened engagements pay no cost. Meta DB has its own Alembic chain that applies at desktop launch.

Q8 — Per-engagement exports. Split (dogfood in engine repo, client in client repo) via engagement_export_dir field vs all in engine repo vs manual command only. Option A approved: nullable engagement_export_dir absolute-path column on the meta DB; dogfood's value set by the v0.5 migration to the computed engine-repo path; client engagement values set at creation time via the New-Engagement dialog. Null disables auto-export. Q1 sub-question resolved here as a downstream implication: engagement record lives in the meta DB row.

Q10b — Engagement status lifecycle. active/paused/archived with free transitions vs domain pattern (candidate/confirmed/deferred) vs two-state (active/archived). Option A approved: active/paused/archived with free transitions, default starter active. Engagement records are direct creations (not propose-verified by a client), so domain's one-way gate doesn't fit. Three states capture real operational lifecycle without ceremony; free transitions match reality (engagements can resume from any state). Soft-delete independent of status.

Q10 — Engagement entity field set (strawman ratification). Ten fields approved: engagement_identifier (ENG-NNN, server-assigned), engagement_code (regex ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}$ mirroring v1's Client.code constraint exactly, case-insensitive unique), engagement_name (required, case-insensitive unique), engagement_purpose (required, plain text 1-2 sentences), engagement_status (enum active/paused/archived, default active, free transitions), engagement_last_opened_at (nullable, for ordering), engagement_export_dir (nullable absolute path, validated when set), engagement_created_at / engagement_updated_at / engagement_deleted_at (inherited base behavior). All prefixed engagement_ per DEC-046. Three deliberate omissions from the domain pattern: no engagement_description (purpose suffices), no engagement_notes (engagement is routing metadata not methodology content; scratchpad notes belong in the per-engagement DB), no engagement_db_path (Q2 chose convention). No relationships in v0.5 (engagement stands alone in the meta DB; per-engagement DB entities don't reference engagement records because engagement is implicit context for everything in the engagement's own DB). Identifier prefix ENG (3 letters, no collision with existing prefixes). Standard endpoint set served from the meta DB (not from the active engagement's DB). No activate endpoint — activation is desktop-side orchestration (kill API → write current_engagement.json → PATCH last_opened_at → launch new API → emit signal). UI deviation justified: engagement is structurally different from the four v0.4 methodology entity types (v2-install-level routing metadata, not domain-scope content), so it doesn't fit the Methodology sidebar group; two needed affordances (switching UI and management UI); exact visual shape deferred to Conversation 2 in coordination with the styling workstream. Dogfood engagement record (CRMBUILDER) field values defined for migration time.

Architectural detail surfaced and recorded. The v0.5 API server connects to two SQLite databases simultaneously: the meta DB (for /engagements/* endpoints) and the active engagement's DB (for everything else). Recorded in multi-engagement-architecture.md §3.10 and engagement.md §3.5.1.

Activation sequence specified. 12-step desktop-side orchestration documented in multi-engagement-architecture.md §4: reachability check, pre-flight Alembic migration, kill API+MCP subprocesses, write current_engagement.json, PATCH engagement_last_opened_at, update in-memory context, launch new API+MCP subprocesses, emit signal. Total elapsed time: a few seconds for a typical switch.

Deliverables produced and committed. Two documents drafted in-conversation: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/multi-engagement-architecture.md (system-level architecture document) and PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/engagement.md (engagement schema spec conforming to methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md). Close-out payload at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_026.json. Apply prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-026.md.

Close-out path chosen. Path 1 (Claude Code apply prompt, mirroring the SES-025 precedent) — the apply prompt POSTs the standard payload via apply_close_out.py.

artifacts_produced: Three documents committed to PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ across two commits on origin/main: (1) styling-design-pass.md (~709 lines, new) — the design pass document; (2) close-out-payloads/ses_027.json (this payload) — SES-027 session record, DEC-087 through DEC-094 decision records, eight decided_in references; (3) prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-027.md — the apply prompt that delivers SES-027 + DEC-087 through DEC-094 + references via Claude Code, mirroring the SES-025 apply-prompt structure. No PI patches this time. The standard apply_close_out.py handles all three sections (session, decisions, references) idempotently.
conversation_reference: Claude.ai conversation on 05-16-26 that opened against the styling Conversation 1 kickoff prompt (PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-conversation-1-kickoff.md, committed earlier same day as part of SES-025 closeout). The conversation drove a structured visual-design discussion through the foundation token layer (density, color, typography, radius/border, elevation, icon library) and through the component visual decisions (sidebar, panel chrome, master pane, detail pane, buttons, form controls, dialogs, About, status/error/warning surfaces, crash banner). Each consequential decision used the eight-element decision template; smaller derivative values were drafted and announced; the design pass document was built incrementally as decisions confirmed. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
in_flight_at_end: Styling Conversation 2 (build planning) queued in a fresh Claude.ai chat — takes styling-design-pass.md as input, produces a styling release PRD, an implementation plan with slice breakdown, and Claude Code slice build prompts. Also settles the version-bundling question (ship as v0.5 bundled with engagement management, or as v0.6 separately) deferred from the workstream plan. v0.5 Conversation 1 (engagement management) may also still be running in parallel — see v0.5-conversation-1-kickoff.md; the two workstreams are coupled only at v0.5's engagement panel (which inherits whatever design tokens have shipped at slice-land time). After both Conversation 2's close, Claude Code slice prompts execute the build; styling build slices follow the strawman five-slice structure in workstream plan §5.3 (Foundation / Governance retrofit / Methodology retrofit / Dialog polish / Closeout). The paper-test (DEC-077) stays deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement record exists.
title: Styling Conversation 1 — design pass: tokens, component visual decisions, application priorities, acceptance criteria
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: the styling Conversation 1 kickoff at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-conversation-1-kickoff.md (authored 05-16-26 14:00 during the SES-025 closeout), against the project default ARCHITECTURE operating mode. The kickoff framed Conversation 1 as design-only — no code, no build planning, no version-bundling settling, no work on engagement management (v0.5 Conversation 1 runs in parallel and owns the data/routing layer). Target deliverable: a single design pass document at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-design-pass.md covering design tokens, component visual decisions, application priorities, and acceptance criteria. The conversation was explicitly framed as visual-iteration-heavy rather than written-PRD-heavy — multiple short rounds (propose → react → revise → propose next) rather than one long writeup.

Pre-flight. Sparse clone of crmbuilder repo with CLAUDE.md, PRDs/, and crmbuilder-v2/ source. Read CLAUDE.md (684 lines) to ground architecture and conventions. Verified SES-025 close-out had applied cleanly: latest session SES-025, latest decision DEC-077, planning items through PI-016. Working tree clean on main. Next available identifiers: SES-027, DEC-087, PI-017. Read the styling workstream plan at styling-workstream-plan.md (227 lines) end to end. Read the v0.4 UI PRD §§4.1-4.7 (panel UI specifications) and §6 (acceptance criteria), and the v0.3 UI PRD §4.1 (ListDetailPanel factory refactor section) to ground the structural pattern that the visual treatment applies to. Pulled DEC-024 / DEC-026 / DEC-037 / DEC-042 / DEC-075 / DEC-076 / DEC-077 from the decisions snapshot — the historical deferral chain (four deferrals through v0.4) plus the parallel-workstream reopening (DEC-076).

Current visual state inventory. Grepped crmbuilder-v2/src for setStyleSheet/QPalette/stylesheet/color/background/font-family/font-size hits — 66 total touchpoints across the v2 source. Three recurring patterns emerged: read-only field treatment (color: #444; background: #f4f4f4) used identically across every methodology panel (Domains, Entities, Processes, CRM Candidates) and base/crud_dialog.py — the closest thing v2 has to a shipping convention. Secondary-text labels (color: #888) used on Sessions, Risks, Topics, Decisions, Planning Items panels for empty-state messages and captions. Navy stub (#1F3864) used in two visible-to-user places: the sidebar stale-indicator dot (via QPainter on a QPixmap) and the error-dialog banner title text. Plus one-off treatments: destructive-button red (#c1272d / disabled #b6868a) on reference-delete and crud-dialog delete buttons; inline error red (#B22222) on validation errors; warning red (same #B22222) on the Processes panel's soft-deleted-domain warning. The crash banner has its own self-contained chrome with white-on-color text — the only branded surface in v0.4. The sidebar (sidebar.py) has zero visual styling: group headers are non-selectable list items with Qt defaults. The About dialog is pure Qt defaults: QFormLayout with bold labels, 16px margins, 12px spacing, 440px min width, no chrome. Both blank canvases for the design pass.

Proposed decision order. Token-layer decisions in upstream-to-downstream sequence: density target (upstream of all spacing tokens), token-naming structure (upstream of color-token naming), brand accent + palette family (upstream of selected-state and primary-button decisions), font family (upstream of all text rendering), radius/border (smaller), elevation/depth (modal-disambiguation), icon library (parallel). Then component visual decisions in container-then-atomic order: sidebar, panel chrome, master pane, detail pane, buttons, form controls, dialogs, About, status/error/warning surfaces, crash banner reconciliation. Then application priorities and acceptance criteria. Estimated 5-10 consequential pause-decisions plus many decide-and-announce drafts.

Decision 1 — Density target. First pause, full eight-element template. Three options: compact (~20-22px row heights, 13px body, IDE-aesthetic — VS Code / JetBrains feel), default (~26-28px rows, 14px body, Qt-native-aligned), comfortable (~30-34px rows, 14-16px body, consumer-SaaS feel). Recommendation was compact, on the reasoning that v2 is a power-user tool with one primary user (Doug) spending substantial daily time inside it; compact recovers screen real estate that pays back in cumulative scan time. The recommendation was overridden by Doug's workstation context: 3× 4K monitors, rarely uses a laptop. At 4K HiDPI, compact density would feel undersized rather than crisp (HiDPI scaling already tightens visual rhythm), and there is no scarcity of screen pixels to optimize for. Default density chosen. Spacing scale fell out as follow-on: 4px base unit with scale 4/8/12/16/20/24/32/40/48; list row 28px; form field 28px; button padding 6×14px with 88px minimum width; panel chrome 16px outer padding; dialog margins 20px outer + 12px between rows + 16px above buttons.

Decision 2 — Token-naming structure. The kickoff flagged dark-mode anticipation at the token-naming layer as a real fork, with weak prior to anticipate. The workstream plan §3.2 explicitly puts dark mode implementation out of scope, deferred to a separate PI if anyone asks. Treated as decide-and-announce rather than full pause: theme-keyed dict structure (TOKENS['light'][key]) over flat tokens. Reasoning: we are building the tokens module from scratch in the build phase; adding the theme-key dict layer costs one extra dict key and one accessor function (~5 LOC) today. The retrofit cost if dark mode ever ships and tokens went flat is non-trivial (rename every color token in code, refactor every consumer). The theme-keyed structure also pays an immediate non-dark-mode dividend by separating data from interface, making future palette experiments (CBM-branded variant, print-friendly variant) trivially additive. No pushback.

Decision 3 — Brand accent and palette family. Pause with full eight-element template. Four options: keep navy #1F3864 (the legacy stub, never deliberately chosen), different blue family (steelier #1F5FBF or brighter Tailwind-like #2563EB), non-blue accent (deep green / warm purple / muted plum), grayscale-only with no chroma accent. Doug chose B (different blue). Follow-up sub-pick: steelier vs brighter — chose the steelier #1F5FBF on the reasoning that it matches the 'serious tool, deliberate choice' read better than the Tailwind-bright value, which leans consumer-SaaS-marketing. Cool grays follow naturally for pairing with cool blue accent. Drafted 9-step neutral scale: neutral.0 #FFFFFF, neutral.50 #F7F9FB, neutral.100 #EEF1F5, neutral.200 #DDE3EA, neutral.300 #C1CAD4, neutral.500 #7A8694, neutral.700 #3F4854, neutral.800 #272D36, neutral.900 #0F1318. The legacy four ad-hoc grays (#f4f4f4, #444, #666, #888) map to the new scale during the styling-build retrofit. Status colors drafted to harmonize with the cool blue accent: danger #C0392B, danger.text #A8281C, warning #B0731A, success #2D7A4D (rare in v2). No pushback on the cool-gray scale or status drafts.

Decision 4 — Font family. Pause with full eight-element template. Four options: system-default per platform (status quo), Inter Variable bundled, other open sans (Geist / IBM Plex Sans / Source Sans / JetBrains Sans), distinctive font (Berkeley Mono / Atkinson Hyperlegible). Recommended Inter; Doug chose B. Drafted size scale: caption 12px / small 13px / body 14px / body_large 16px / heading_3 18px / heading_2 22px / heading_1 28px (reserved). Weight scale (Inter Variable axis): regular 400 / medium 500 / semibold 600 / bold 700. Line-height scale: tight 1.2 (headings) / normal 1.45 (body) / relaxed 1.6 (long-form prose). Monospace: JetBrains Mono Variable bundled for identifier columns, code surfaces, About dialog path values. All loaded via QFontDatabase.addApplicationFont() at app startup. Total binary cost ~530 KB.

Decision 5 — Elevation and depth posture. Pause with full eight-element template. Three options: fully flat throughout (current behavior, no shadows), elevation only on modals (one elevation tier, applied via QGraphicsDropShadowEffect which is Qt's built-in effect — within the workstream plan's QSS-supplemented-by-built-in-effects posture, not custom paintEvent which is out of scope), broader elevation tiers (modals + popup combos + selected list rows + focused buttons — multiple z-tiers). Recommended B; Doug chose B. Drafted values: shadow.dialog at Y-offset 4px, X-offset 0px, blur radius 16px, color color.neutral.900 at 25% alpha. Plus overlay.modal_backdrop — full-window overlay at color.neutral.900 8% alpha, fade-in ~150ms when modal opens. Single elevation token applied uniformly to every QDialog subclass.

Decision 6 — Icon library. Pause, compressed template (narrower than prior token-layer decisions). Three options: Qt-built-in / system-theme icons via QIcon::fromTheme (inconsistent on Linux, empty on macOS/Windows without explicit fallback), Phosphor (~7,000 icons, 6 weight variants, MIT, warmer rounded aesthetic), Lucide (~1,400 icons, single regular stroke, ISC, minimal/restrained aesthetic). Recommended C; Doug chose C. Drafted bundling approach: store SVGs at crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/assets/icons/lucide/{kebab-case-name}.svg; small loader helper wraps QSvgRenderer to produce QIcon at requested size with runtime color tinting via the design token system. Initial wave of ~10-15 icons covering Edit, Delete, Restore, Go-to-references, Copy, Add, Close, chevrons, alert, error, check.

Section 1 of the design pass document (tokens) was now substantially complete and the design pass file was created on disk at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/styling-design-pass.md with §1.1 through §1.6 filled in or drafted, §2 stubbed for component decisions, §3 and §4 stubbed. The file was committed-ready in the container's working clone; no commits or pushes yet.

Decision 7 — Sidebar visual treatment. Pause, compressed template. The central decision was selected-entry treatment; the rest of the sidebar fell out as drafts. Three options for selected state: tinted background only (no accent bar — risk of blurring with hover), full-fill with accent color and white text (heavy in a 12+ item list), left-edge accent bar + tinted background (modern dev-tool convention — VS Code / JetBrains / Linear / shadcn). Recommended A (left bar); Doug chose A. Drafted sidebar specifics: container background color.neutral.100 distinct from panel color.neutral.50 with 1px hairline right border; sidebar width 220px; group headers in font.size.caption font.weight.semibold color.neutral.500 with +0.04em letter-spacing; entries with 32px height, font.size.body, padding space.2 × space.3; hover at color.neutral.200 background with no bar (distinct from selected); focused state matches selected plus 1px focus ring; stale-indicator dot retained at 8px circle but color flips from legacy navy to color.accent.default. No pushback on drafts.

§2.2 Panel chrome — drafted with one small announce-decision (no panel header — sidebar already indicates which panel is active, adding a header strip would be redundant labeling and consume vertical space; pushback welcome but none received). Drafts: panel content background color.neutral.50; outer padding space.4; master/detail split via existing QSplitter at 45/55 default proportion, space.3 handle width with color.neutral.100 background and color.neutral.300 hairline divider down the center.

Decision 8 — Master pane visual posture. Pause, full eight-element template. Three options: minimal/quiet (Linear-issue-list aesthetic — no banding, no dividers, header has subtle background, hover and selected tints only), structured/banded (admin-tool aesthetic — alternating row shading, row dividers, prominent header background, sidebar-mirrored selected state with left bar), middle (no banding but row dividers, clear header background, sidebar-mirrored selected state). Recommended C; Doug chose C. Reasoning: v2's master panes have low row counts (Decisions 77, References ~150, Sessions 25, others <30) and ~22 rows visible at default density on 4K — alternating banding is most valuable at 50+ visible rows where the eye loses its place; for v2 it would be visual noise. Going fully minimal would feel under-organized for a structured data table; middle threads the needle. Drafted specifics: row height 28px; row dividers 1px hairline color.neutral.200; column header background color.neutral.100 with bottom hairline color.neutral.200 and font.weight.semibold color.neutral.700 text; row hover color.neutral.100; row selected gets 3px left accent bar + color.accent.subtle background + color.neutral.900 medium-weight text (matches sidebar selected vocabulary — same design vocabulary across both surfaces, custom QStyledItemDelegate subclass shared across all panels); soft-deleted rows render in color.neutral.500 text with leading Lucide trash-2 icon at 14px in identifier column; empty state centered with primary 'No records yet' line and per-panel secondary hint line below in color.neutral.500. Identifier column renders in font.family.mono (JetBrains Mono) at font.size.small so identifiers like DEC-077 align cleanly by numeric suffix. Tree view variant (Topics panel) inherits the same treatment with Lucide chevron-right/chevron-down at 12px replacing Qt's default branch indicator and space.4 indentation per level.

Decision 9 — Detail pane. Pause, compressed (mostly draftable). The pause focused on ReferencesSection visual treatment; the smaller question of form-row label placement (label-above vs label-left) was decide-and-announce (chose label-above on the reasoning that Default density gives enough vertical room and label-above scans faster when labels vary in length — 'Identifier' vs 'Process classification'). ReferencesSection options: sub-sectioned plain list with kind header + entry rows below (A); card-style per relationship kind with bordered cards stacking vertically (B); badge/pill rendering per entry with kind as tiny caption (C). Recommended A; Doug chose A. Reasoning: ReferencesSection is reference material the user scans, not a primary editing surface — plain lists scan fastest, scale gracefully to many references, and do not compete visually with the rest of the detail pane. Cards over-bound a section already inside a well-bounded detail pane; pills compress too aggressively for typical entry length. Drafted entry rendering: identifier in font.family.mono at font.size.small color.neutral.700; title text after identifier in font.size.body color.neutral.800; space.3 between identifier and title; space.1 vertical padding per row; hover tint color.neutral.50; right-click context menu with Delete reference / Go to {target identifier}. Add-reference button as text-only with Lucide plus icon + label 'Add reference' in color.accent.default. Drafted remaining detail-pane elements: form-row label in font.size.small font.weight.medium color.neutral.700 with space.1 below before field; field height 28px; row spacing space.3; required-field marker as Lucide asterisk at 10px color.danger.text immediately after label text (no 'Required' word); editable field state coverage (default / focused / error / disabled); read-only field treatment (color.neutral.100 background, no border, color.neutral.700 text — visually distinct from disabled which has color.neutral.200 border + color.neutral.300 text); status combo with valid-transitions hint caption below; notes collapsible toggle with Lucide chevron + label; section grouping space.6 between major sections with no dividers; action button row anchored at bottom with right-aligned Cancel + Save and left-aligned Delete.

§2.5 Buttons — fully drafted, no pause needed. Five categories × five states. Primary (color.accent.default fill with color.neutral.0 text), Secondary (transparent with color.neutral.300 border), Destructive (color.danger.default fill — retires the legacy #c1272d and disabled #b6868a treatments), Text/Link (transparent with color.accent.default text in font.weight.medium for 'Add reference' and similar affordances), Icon-only (28×28 with color.neutral.700 icon at rest, color.neutral.100 hover background, tooltip required). All buttons share padding space.1 × space.3 (effectively 6×14), radius.subtle, minimum width 88px so Save/Cancel line up in dialog button rows. Per-state visuals fully specified.

§2.6 Form controls — fully drafted, no pause needed. Text input, multi-line text (80px minimum height), combo box with Lucide chevron-down right-side affordance and full dropdown popup treatment (color.accent.subtle background on current value, color.neutral.100 on hover), checkbox (16×16 box with color.accent.default fill and Lucide check icon when checked), radio button reserved (no current v2 surface uses), date/time picker reserved (no current v2 surface uses), spin box reserved, scrollbars OS-default per kickoff settled-by-convention guidance, tab order top-to-bottom with initial focus on first editable field not Save button.

§2.7 Dialogs — drafted with one announce-decision. The substantive question was whether dialog bodies show an internal context strip identifying the record being edited; decide-and-announce was yes for record-editing dialogs (not for create-new, About, or delete-confirm). Reasoning: OS title bar text ('Edit Decision DEC-076') is variably visible across Linux compositors and macOS focus states; a small internal strip inside the dialog body anchors the user without requiring them to look at OS chrome. Context strip drafted: anchored at top of dialog body flush with OS title bar, background color.neutral.100, padding space.3, identifier in font.family.mono at font.size.small color.neutral.700 font.weight.medium followed by space.3 horizontal then record name/title in font.size.body color.neutral.800 (truncates with ellipsis), hairline color.neutral.200 below. Remaining dialog drafts: container background color.neutral.0, inner padding space.5, radius.dialog 6px where platform renders, drop shadow per shadow.dialog and backdrop overlay per overlay.modal_backdrop. Action button row anchored at bottom with right-aligned Cancel + primary and left-aligned Destructive. Delete-confirm dialogs specifically: no context strip, single paragraph body with deletion target inline at font.line.relaxed 1.6, Destructive button right-aligned with Cancel to its left.

Decision 10 — About dialog showcase treatment. Pause, compressed template. Three options: apply design system with no special treatment (under-leverages the natural identity moment), modest showcase with wordmark + tagline header but no logo (visible identity without requiring brand-mark authoring), full showcase with custom brand mark in addition to wordmark + tagline (requires authoring a logo). Recommended B; Doug chose B. Reasoning: the About dialog is the natural identity moment in the application and treating it like every other dialog feels under-leveraged. But brand-mark authoring is a separate subjective design problem unsuitable for remote conversation resolution, and v2 has no established mark to evolve. Modest showcase threads the needle. Drafted: wordmark 'CRMBuilder v2' in font.size.heading_2 font.weight.semibold color.neutral.900; tagline below at font.size.small color.neutral.500 — proposed 'Declarative CRM deployment and methodology authoring' with alternative tagline candidates noted in §2.8 as refineable at build time. Metadata table restructured from QFormLayout to a vertical two-line-per-row list (label above value, value selectable so users can copy paths). Single secondary 'Close' button right-aligned at bottom. Minimum width preserved at 440px from current code.

§2.9 Status / error / warning surfaces — fully drafted, no pause needed. Inline form-field errors below the field at font.size.caption color.danger.text with no icon (the field's red border carries the visual signal). Inline panel-level warnings as a single-row callout with Lucide circle-alert icon at 14px in color.warning.default + warning text at font.size.small color.warning.default — the existing _WARNING_STYLE = 'color: #B22222;' on panels/processes.py updates to use color.warning.default (amber, not danger red — the soft-deleted-domain message is informational warning, not error). Error dialog (dialogs/error.py) header retoken: leading Lucide circle-x at 18px in color.danger.default + title in font.size.heading_3 font.weight.semibold color.danger.text. Retires the legacy _BANNER_STYLE = 'color: #1F3864; font-weight: bold;'. Success affirmations rare in v2 — reserved pattern with Lucide check icon and color.success.default treatment if introduced. Status indicators include the stale-dot (covered in §2.1) and reserved future status badges for singleton-selected CRM Candidates (pill rendering at radius.subtle).

§2.10 Crash banner reconciliation — decide-and-announce. Two options: re-skin into the design system (use color.danger.default from the new system, retire the bespoke navy/white treatment) versus keep outside the design system as the one 'this is exceptional' surface that doesn't follow normal app rules. Re-skin chosen. Reasoning: leaving the banner outside means permanent parallel maintenance for one surface; folding in costs nothing visually since color.danger.default is unambiguously red and reads as alarming; future style adjustments propagate automatically. Drafted: banner background color.danger.default, text color.neutral.0 in font.size.body font.weight.medium, padding space.3 vertical × space.4 horizontal, leading Lucide circle-alert at 16px in color.neutral.0. Banner buttons treated as semi-transparent white-on-color: default at 15% alpha background, hover at 30% alpha, pressed at 45% alpha. Pushback welcome but none received.

§3 Application priorities — drafted directly, no pause. Distinguishes high-attention surfaces (per-surface tuning required, custom widget code beyond QSS) from passive-absorption surfaces (QSS plus token mapping only). High attention: sidebar (custom delegate or paint-event override for selected-state left bar), master pane (shared QStyledItemDelegate for left bar + soft-deleted treatment), ReferencesSection (new layout pattern), About dialog (header block restructure from QFormLayout to QVBoxLayout), crash banner (button-state retuning), modal elevation infrastructure (QGraphicsDropShadowEffect setup + backdrop overlay widget). Passive absorption: panel chrome, detail pane form treatment, buttons, form controls, standard dialogs, inline status/error/warning treatments. Suggested implementation order across the build phase: foundation (tokens module, font loaders, icon loader, base widget refactors, elevation infrastructure, About dialog as smallest end-to-end exercise) → sidebar → master pane delegate → governance panel retrofit → methodology panel retrofit → dialog polish → status/error/warning + crash banner → closeout. Order approximate; Conversation 2 may restructure based on file-conflict topology with v0.5 parallel work.

§4 Acceptance criteria — drafted directly, no pause. Five categories: token completeness (every category in §1 has concrete values, no TBDs, theme-keyed structure internally consistent, status colors harmonize with accent), component completeness (every component class has prose visual decision, no stubs remain, state coverage complete for all interactive components, selected-state vocabulary consistent across surfaces), contradiction check (no conflicting values, density-derived sizes mutually consistent), accessibility floor (WCAG AA contrast verified for every text-on-background combination — body text 14.4:1 passes AAA, secondary 4.62:1 passes AA, accent on white 4.61:1 passes AA, white-on-accent 4.66:1 passes AA for body, white-on-danger ~5.1:1 passes AA, warning on white borderline at 12px caption size noted for build-time refinement — focus indicators visible on every interactive element, color is not the sole signal for state), implementer readiness (implementer can produce QSS without further questions, legacy-to-token mapping explicit). Out of scope documented in §4.6: dark mode color values (structure supports but values not authored), animation, icon SVG authoring beyond initial set, cross-platform polish testing (Windows-specific deferred per workstream plan), full accessibility audit beyond WCAG AA contrast floor (deferred to separate workstream if requested).

Document built incrementally during the conversation, ending at 709 lines, four main sections fully filled (§1 tokens, §2 component decisions across ten subsections, §3 application priorities, §4 acceptance criteria), preamble with revision control and change log per project standards, MM-DD-YY HH:MM timestamp format.

Close-out planning. Eight consequential decisions to formalize as DEC-087 through DEC-094: density (default), brand accent + cool-gray family, theme-keyed token structure, font stack (Inter + JetBrains Mono), modal elevation only, Lucide icon library, selected-state visual vocabulary (left accent bar + tinted background applied to both sidebar and master pane), About dialog modest-showcase treatment. Smaller decide-and-announce items (no panel header, internal dialog context strip, label-above forms, ReferencesSection sub-sectioned plain list, master pane middle posture, button drafts, form control drafts, status surface drafts, crash banner re-skin) fold into the design pass document but do not warrant separate DEC records — they derive from the eight foundational ones or from the workstream plan's pre-existing scope. No new planning items: all deferred work either folds into existing PI-001 (the parent for the whole styling pass) or is documented as out-of-scope in §4.6 of the design pass. Path 1 close-out chosen (apply prompt via Claude Code, mirroring the SES-025 precedent) over Path 2 (typing each record into the desktop New Session dialog) — apply prompt produces a reviewable artifact.

artifacts_produced: Nine deliverables committed to origin/main in one commit by Claude per the in-sandbox close-out convention: (1) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.5.md (release PRD, 425 lines, draft pending approval); (2) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-v0.5-implementation-plan.md (slice breakdown, 437 lines, draft pending approval); (3) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-A-foundation-and-dogfood-migration.md (slice A build prompt: meta DB + ActiveEngagementContext + two-database API + lazy migration + dogfood migration + sidebar Engagements group + refresh service extension + .gitignore extensions); (4) -B-engagement-schema-and-api.md (slice B: engagement repository + eight REST endpoints + validation + JSON snapshot hook + client extensions); (5) -C-engagement-management-panel.md (slice C: management panel + CRUD dialogs + forbid-active-delete with last-engagement edge case + refresh service registration + sidebar entry); (6) -D-engagement-switching.md (slice D: top-strip widget + picker dropdown + activation overlay + 12-step ActivationWorker + single-gesture NewEngagementDialog + slice-C placeholder rewiring); (7) -E-closeout.md (slice E: version 0.5.0 bump + README release note + end-to-end integration smoke + final regression pass + manual smoke checklist); (8) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/close-out-payloads/ses_029.json (this payload); (9) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-apply-close-out-ses-029.md (apply prompt for this payload).
conversation_reference: Claude.ai conversation on 05-16-26 that opened against the seed prompt at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/v0.5-conversation-2-kickoff.md. Translated v0.5 Conversation 1's two deliverables (multi-engagement-architecture.md and methodology-schema-specs/engagement.md) into a build plan. Settled the ten Conversation-2-deferred questions cataloged in the kickoff §5.1 (the two UI-shape questions for switching and management affordances; single-gesture creation+activation behavior; soft-delete-on-active-engagement posture; default value for engagement_export_dir on new engagements; the engagement_last_opened_at PATCH path during the switching dance; engagement panel refresh trigger; activation-sequence retry/timeout values; slice placement of the dogfood migration; and meta DB export location). Produced ui-PRD-v0.5.md, ui-v0.5-implementation-plan.md, and the five slice build prompts at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-{A..E}-*.md. Three-way numbering collision with the parallel PI-001 styling workstream resolved at closeout: styling Conversation 1 (SES-027) close-out applied first claiming DEC-087-094; styling Conversation 2 ran in parallel with this conversation and claimed SES-028 plus DEC-095-097 for its v0.6 PRD authoring; this conversation therefore claims SES-029 and DEC-098-104. Mechanical bookkeeping per the v0.4 SES-016 → SES-017 precedent. No transcript preserved per DEC-025.
in_flight_at_end: Slice A is the next executable Claude Code prompt. Doug runs it at his local terminal: clone or pull crmbuilder repo, open Claude Code in the repo, instruct Claude Code to execute the slice A prompt. Slice A produces the foundation infrastructure plus the dogfood migration; after slice A completes and Doug pushes, slice B is queued; and so on through slice E. Status remains 'v0.4 complete' until slice E lands and the status entity is operator-updated. The PI-001 styling workstream's v0.6 release runs in parallel per DEC-095's separate-release sequencing; v0.6's slice C retrofits the engagement panel introduced in v0.5 slice C, alongside the existing twelve panels — v0.5 ships first with Qt-default styling, v0.6 retrofits tokens onto all panels including the engagement panel. The User Process Guide v0.2 update is deferred to v0.6 per PRD §2 Out of Scope. The paper-test workstream (DEC-077) remains deferred until v0.5 ships and a CBM engagement is created via single-gesture flow.
title: v0.5 Conversation 2 — build planning (release PRD, implementation plan, five slice build prompts)
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/v0.5-conversation-2-kickoff.md against the project default ARCHITECTURE operating mode.

Pre-flight. Read crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (684 lines; particular attention to the API envelope contract {data, meta, errors} added at commit 9b6fcdc and the prefixed-identifier client-side-computation rule); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/multi-engagement-architecture.md from Conversation 1 (the 12-step activation sequence in §4 and the two-database API server detail in §3.10 plus the five Conversation-2-deferred questions in §6.1); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/engagement.md (the engagement entity schema and the five Conversation-2-deferred questions in §3.8.1); PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/v0.5-engagement-management-workstream-plan.md (the four-slice strawman in §5 and the alternative slicings); ui-PRD-v0.4.md and ui-v0.4-implementation-plan.md (shape templates for the v0.5 release deliverables); the User Process Guide / Engagement Playbook v0.1 at PRDs/process/v2-user-process-guide.md committed at 7885cfe (data layer described as a single SQLite file v2.db; v0.5 introduces multi-engagement routing, so v0.2 update is needed but deferred to v0.6 per the in-flight workstream).

Surfaced numbering-collision context. The parallel PI-001 styling workstream's Conversation 1 (SES-027) closed earlier the same day at 05-16-26; its close-out payload at close-out-payloads/ses_027.json claims SES-027 plus DEC-087 through DEC-094 (eight decisions covering density, color palette, typography, theme keying, modal elevation, icon library Lucide, selected-state vocabulary, and the styling design pass approval). The styling Conversation 2 ran in parallel and committed ui-PRD-v0.6.md plus ui-v0.6-implementation-plan.md mid-way through this conversation; styling Conversation 2 claims SES-028 plus DEC-095-097 (version-bundling as v0.6 separate from v0.5, six-slice structure A-F, per-slice screenshot + WCAG check acceptance pattern). This conversation's payload therefore claims SES-029 plus DEC-098-104 — collision resolution applied at close after the styling apply landed.

Q1+Q2 (consequential decision, paired). UI affordance placement for switching and for management. Three options framed: A — top-strip switching widget + Engagements sidebar group with one entry + dual paths to management; B — sidebar-only with everything in the Engagements group; C — modal-only switching with sidebar group for management. Option A approved. Rationale: switching is a frequent action that needs always-visible affordance distinct from the sidebar's content-navigation function; the Engagements sidebar group entry is the discoverability path for management; dual paths to the management panel (sidebar entry + 'Manage engagements...' picker footer) reduce friction without creating navigational ambiguity. The picker dropdown lives below the top-strip with engagements ordered by engagement_last_opened_at descending, paused/archived sorted to bottom in muted color, active marked with Lucide check icon; footer item opens management panel. Sidebar group positioned above Governance per the v2-install-level scope distinction.

Q3 (consequential decision). Single-gesture engagement creation+activation. Two options: A — one click runs POST + DB file creation + activation behind the scenes with graceful failure recovery; B — two-gesture path with explicit 'Create' then 'Activate' affordances. Option A approved. The New Engagement dialog runs three sequential operations on Submit (POST /engagements → desktop creates per-engagement DB file with Alembic at head → desktop initiates 12-step activation). Three-label progress indicator advances through the operations. Activation failure after both creates succeed surfaces inline with 'Try switching now' / 'Stay in <previous>' affordances; engagement record persists. Validation errors at each stage roll back appropriately. Rationale: matches user mental model (creating an engagement implies starting work in it); the failure-recovery affordances handle the one realistic partial-success state cleanly; the alternative two-gesture flow would force every creator to choose 'now or later' twice which is friction without payoff.

Q9 (consequential decision, refined from kickoff strawman). Slice breakdown. Workstream-plan strawman had four slices (foundation+migration combined, engagement entity panel, switching, closeout); alternative granular framing had six slices (config foundation, dogfood migration, schema+access+REST, panel UI, switching, closeout). Decided five slices: A foundation+migration combined (because foundation alone is non-functional intermediate state), B engagement schema+access layer+REST API, C engagement management panel UI, D switching mechanism+top-strip+picker+single-gesture creation+activation flow, E closeout. Slice A monitored for size at execution time; A1/A2 split fallback documented. Rationale for splitting B from C: B's REST API surface (eight standard endpoints with validation) plus C's panel UI (master/detail panel plus CRUD dialogs plus forbid-active-delete) are each substantial; combining them produces an oversize prompt. Rationale for D as one slice rather than splitting picker from activation worker: the picker and activation worker are tightly coupled (picker click triggers activation worker; activation overlay binds to worker progress signals; the single-gesture flow uses the activation worker as its third step); splitting would require defining a fake activation API in slice C/D and a backfill in the next slice.

Decide-and-announce decisions. Q4 — Forbid soft-delete on active engagement: delete dialog refuses with inline redirect to switch first; last-engagement edge case uses 'create another first' wording. The drift-recovery path in spec §3.4.5 remains as safety net. Q5 — Null default for engagement_export_dir on new engagements: dialog field empty by default with 'Optional — leave blank to disable auto-export' placeholder; null disables auto-export with log warning at activation; the dogfood migration explicitly sets a non-null value for CRMBUILDER (the engine-repo db-export/ path computed at migration time).

Operational decisions (just decided per the two-part test outcome). Q6 — engagement_last_opened_at PATCH path during the switching dance: Option B (defer PATCH until after new API subprocess is up) chosen over Option A (desktop opens meta DB directly during the kill-relaunch dance). Respects DEC-019's API-only principle for recurring operations (the dogfood migration's direct-DB-access exception is a one-shot first-launch thing; activation happens every switch). The PATCH lagging by the few hundred milliseconds between API health-check success and signal emission is imperceptible; PATCH is idempotent so health-check failure leaves the timestamp unchanged with no other consequence. Architecture doc §4 step ordering amended (step 7 moves to between steps 9 and 11; renumbered 12-step sequence documented in PRD §3 architecture summary). summary: Two-commit Claude Code session. Commit one (a4c8b35) executed the slice A launcher-wiring prompt as written: bootstrap_meta_db() at API startup, dogfood-migration trigger at desktop startup with QProgressDialog and hard-fail UX, three regression tests. Discovered a SQLite race during full-suite run (create_all(checkfirst=True) not atomic against eight parallel test-client TestClients) and absorbed it with a narrow OperationalError catch at the call site, preserving slice A's helper untouched per the prompt's 'do not modify slice A helpers' guardrail. Manual smoke surfaced a second defect: per-engagement endpoints 500 against Doug's running API. Diagnosed as a slice D gap — initial UI launch never sets CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH because activation only fires on user-driven engagement switches. Investigation surfaced a third latent bug upstream: access/meta_db.meta_db_path() and migration/lazy_migration.engagement_db_path() derive their locations from s.db_path.parent, which silently produces wrong nested paths when db_path is in the post-activation engagements/{code}.db shape (the slice E end-to-end test masks this with no-op subprocess managers; the activation worker would have hit it in production). Commit two (3467764) closes both: ui/app.py.main now routes the API at the active engagement before lifecycle.start(), and a new data_dir() helper in access/meta_db.py normalises both layouts back to data/ so the meta DB and sibling engagement DBs always resolve correctly regardless of which file db_path names. Both commits pushed; operator API restarted with the env var explicitly set; UI verified green.
title: v0.5 slice A follow-up (launcher wiring) + slice D follow-up (route API at active engagement, fix latent data_dir derivation) — two-commit Claude Code session
topics_covered:
Pre-flight per the prompt: git pull (already up to date at start), confirmed working tree clean, identity Doug Bower <doug@dougbower.com> (prompt's noted expectation of dbower44022@users.noreply.github.com was stale against the actual recent-commit convention; matched the convention). Reading order: CLAUDE.md v2 section; UI PRD v0.5 §5.4 (dogfood migration at first launch); slice A api/main.py, ui/app.py, access/meta_db.py, migration/dogfood_v0_5.py; slice E end-to-end test for fixture pattern. Pre-flight noted Doug's local data dir still contained v2.db (prompt expected it absent post-manual-migration); proceeded per the 'reasonable call and continue' direction.

Step 1 — API startup hook (api/main.py). Added bootstrap_meta_db() call before init_meta_db_pool(), keeping the prior run_meta_migrations() try/except in place. First pass shipped bootstrap_meta_db() unguarded.

Step 2 — desktop startup migration trigger (ui/app.py). Introduced _run_dogfood_migration_if_needed(log) helper invoked between build_application() and splash.show(). Wraps the synchronous migration in an indeterminate QProgressDialog with no cancel affordance per slice A spec; on failure surfaces a critical QMessageBox naming the .pre-v0.5-backup recovery path and returns _MIGRATION_FAILED_EXIT_CODE (=1).

Step 3 — integration test (tests/crmbuilder_v2/integration/test_launcher_wiring.py, new file). Three regression tests: test_api_startup_creates_meta_db drives create_app() and asserts engagements.db materialises; test_desktop_launcher_runs_migration_when_needed drives the helper against a freshly-seeded v0.4-state fixture and asserts the post-migration filesystem shape; test_desktop_launcher_is_noop_when_already_migrated drives a second invocation and asserts no duplicate row or file rewrite. Fixture pattern adapted from tests/crmbuilder_v2/integration/test_v0_5_end_to_end.py (inline v0.4-shape v2.db seeding rather than the absent fixtures/v0_4_state_v2.db file the prompt sketched).

First full-suite run regressed two previously-passing tests: tests/crmbuilder_v2/api/test_domains_api.py and test_entities_api.py — both test_concurrent_posts_get_distinct_identifiers. Isolated repro confirmed 4/5 to 6/10 failure rate when run together, never in isolation. Traceback fingered create_all(checkfirst=True) racing past has_table on SQLite when eight TestClient(create_app()) instances spin up in parallel — the loser hits 'table engagements already exists'. Narrow OperationalError catch around bootstrap_meta_db() in api/main.py absorbed the race; subsequent 10/10 isolated reruns confirmed clean and the full suite returned 1232 passed (+3 vs. baseline 1229). Lint via ruff --fix sorted imports in api/main.py. Stashed local changes, git pull --rebase origin main (one intervening v0.6 slice E prompt commit, unrelated), restored, committed as a4c8b35.

Manual smoke against Doug's machine surfaced a separate failure: UI launched, lifecycle adopted the externally-running API at port 8765, and per-engagement endpoints (/decisions, /charter/versions) returned HTTP 500 while /engagements and /health returned 200. Inspection showed Doug's data dir had three files in place — v2.db (empty: no alembic_version, no decisions, no charter), engagements/CRMBUILDER.db (alembic head 0010_v0_4_create_crm_candidates_table, 101 decisions, 2 charter), engagements.db (meta DB with the CRMBUILDER row). The running API had no CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH set in its environment, so Settings.db_path defaulted to data/v2.db and SQLAlchemy lazy-create had materialised an empty file there at first connection. Confirmed not caused by the just-shipped launcher-wiring fix (the running API was started before the commit and inherits no env var management from it).

Root cause investigation. Slice D wired engagement switching through ActivationWorker, whose build_lifecycle_managers._launch_api correctly sets CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH to engagement_db_path(target.engagement_code) before lifecycle.start(). But the initial UI launch never triggers activation — it loads ActiveEngagementContext from current_engagement.json, hands the UI a populated context, then ServerLifecycle.start() either adopts an externally-running API or QProcess-spawns a fresh one. Neither path touches the env var. So a clean install or any session-restart presents the same symptom; Doug's case was the externally-running-API variant.

While sketching Option A (set CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH in ui/app.py from active_engagement.engagement_code()), surfaced a second latent defect upstream: access/meta_db.meta_db_path() and migration/lazy_migration.engagement_db_path() both compute their location as s.db_path.parent / 'engagements.db' (or '.../engagements/{code}.db'). Correct when db_path is data/v2.db (parent = data, derives correctly) but silently wrong when db_path is data/engagements/CRMBUILDER.db (parent = data/engagements, derives data/engagements/engagements.db for the meta DB and data/engagements/engagements/CRMBUILDER.db for sibling engagement DBs). The activation worker has been setting db_path to that shape since slice D landed; the slice E end-to-end test masks the bug with no-op subprocess managers (the real _launch_api is never invoked). A real production spawn after activation would have hit it. Verified with a one-liner probe (CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH=/tmp/x/data/engagements/CRMBUILDER.db → meta_db_path resolves wrong).

Operator gate. Presented three options: narrow Option A only (trades /decisions 500 for /engagements 500 on fresh spawn — net zero on Doug's case); recommended Option A + fix data_dir derivation (closes the latent activation-worker bug as a side benefit); or hold off and design a proper fix (per-request engagement routing per DEC-080's committed multi-tenant migration trajectory). Operator chose option two.

Implementation. (1) access/meta_db.py — new public data_dir() helper that returns parent.parent when parent.name == 'engagements', else parent; meta_db_path() delegates to it. Backward-compatible against every existing test. (2) migration/lazy_migration.py — engagement_db_path() imports data_dir() and uses it the same way; layout-invariant. (3) ui/app.py — new _route_api_at_active_engagement(active, log) helper invoked between active_engagement.load_from_disk() (which still reads current_engagement.json under the default data-dir resolution) and lifecycle.start(). On non-None engagement_code, sets the env var and resets settings + engine caches so this process and any QProcess.start child see the new value. No-op for fresh-install or post-deactivation. Removed an unused get_settings import flagged by ruff.

Tests. Added test_data_dir_normalises_both_layouts (parametrized across v2.db and engagements/CRMBUILDER.db shapes) to tests/crmbuilder_v2/access/test_meta_db_connection.py. Added test_route_api_at_active_engagement_sets_env (drives the helper against the freshly-migrated workspace and asserts CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH + get_settings + meta_db_path post-state) and test_route_api_at_active_engagement_noop_without_active (asserts no env mutation for the empty-context case) to test_launcher_wiring.py. Full suite returned 1236 passed (+4 vs. prior commit, +7 vs. baseline). Lint clean. Stashed, rebased (one intervening v0.5 build-execution closeout prompt commit, unrelated), restored, committed as 3467764.

Pushed both commits together (edae295..3467764). Killed the misconfigured running API (auto-classifier blocked the first attempt because the agent did not spawn the process; succeeded after operator authorization). Restarted with CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH explicitly set against engagements/CRMBUILDER.db. Verified: /decisions → 101 rows, /charter/versions → 2 rows, /engagements → 1 row, all 200. Operator confirmed UI working again.

Five categories of work executed on top of slice A's foundation: engagement dataclass fleshed out from the slice-A stub with to_dict() and from_row() helpers; access-layer repository with eight standard methods against the meta DB pool with full validation per methodology-schema-specs/engagement.md §3.5; REST API surface with eight standard endpoints consuming the slice-A get_meta_db dependency, replacing the slice-A stub healthcheck; JSON snapshot hook that writes to the meta DB regenerate db-export/meta/engagements.json per the v0.3+ DEC-022 pattern; and client extensions with eight new methods on StorageClient mirroring the v0.4 domain client pattern.

Validation per engagement.md §3.5 in the access-layer repository: identifier regex ^ENG-\d{3}$; code regex ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{1,9}$ mirroring v1's Client.code constraint; case-insensitive uniqueness on code and name; free status transitions across active/paused/archived; immutable code post-creation; export_dir must be absolute existing writable directory when set; soft-delete semantics with restore round-trip; collision-safe identifier auto-assignment using SAVEPOINT retry per the prefixed-identifier client-side-computation rule.

New modules created: access/engagement.py (eight-method repository); access/meta_exporter.py (write_engagements_snapshot atomic write to db-export/meta/engagements.json per DEC-022 pattern); api/routers/engagements.py (eight standard endpoints with envelope-shape preservation: POST returns 201; PUT/PATCH/DELETE/POST-restore return 200; preserves the slice-A healthcheck for backward compat). Modified: access/engagement_models.py (extended slice-A stub with to_dict() / from_row()); api/schemas.py (added EngagementCreateIn, EngagementReplaceIn, EngagementPatchIn Pydantic models); ui/client.py (eight engagement methods); migration/dogfood_v0_5.py (rewired to write_engagements_snapshot for consistency with slice B's hook).

Test suite: 54 new tests added — 27 access-layer tests covering happy paths, validation rules (code format, name uniqueness, status enum, export_dir), status transitions, immutable code, soft-delete idempotency, restore round-trip, identifier auto-assignment, list ordering, snapshot regeneration; 23 API endpoint tests covering all eight endpoints plus envelope-shape verification, 422 validation, 409 collision, 404 not-found. Full non-UI suite green: 600 passed, 1 skipped (up from 546 at slice A close).

After this slice the engagement REST API is operable end-to-end via direct API calls or external scripts. The CRMBUILDER row created by the slice-A dogfood migration is exercisable via GET / PUT / PATCH / DELETE; new engagement records can be created via POST; identifier auto-assignment works (POST /engagements with engagement_identifier omitted assigns ENG-NNN). No UI exposes the API yet — that lands in slice C.

title: v0.5 slice C — engagement management panel UI
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-C-engagement-management-panel.md. Four categories of work executed on top of slices A and B: the engagement management panel as a ListDetailPanel subclass registered as the single entry in the Engagements sidebar group (the empty group container was introduced in slice A); CRUD dialogs as EntityCrudDialog subclasses; the delete dialog with forbid-active-engagement behavior per PRD §5.6 including the only-engagement edge case; and refresh-service registration subscribing the panel to both the db-export/meta/engagements.json file-watch signal and the active_engagement_changed Qt signal from ActiveEngagementContext.

Engagement panel (ui/panels/engagements.py): master pane with five sortable columns per PRD §5.1 — Identifier (mono 80px), Code (mono 100px), Name (body flexible), Status (body with status-aware coloring active/paused/archived), Last Opened (relative-date formatted via format_relative_date helper handling 'N minutes/hours/days ago' / 'YYYY-MM-DD' / '—' cases). Default sort Last Opened descending with soft-deleted rows sorting to the bottom. Soft-deleted row treatment in neutral.500 text with prepended Lucide trash-2 icon, gated behind a 'Show soft-deleted' checkbox that toggles the panel's client.list_engagements(include_deleted=True) call. title: v0.5 slice D — engagement switching, top-strip, picker, single-gesture creation+activation
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.5-D-engagement-switching.md. Five categories of work executed on top of slices A through C: the top-strip widget at the top of the sidebar container (always visible, shows active engagement's name with code in parentheses, clicking opens the picker); the picker dropdown (live engagements ordered by engagement_last_opened_at descending, paused and archived rendered muted and sorted to the bottom, active marked with a check icon, footer 'Manage engagements...' item); the activation worker as a QThread implementing the 12-step activation sequence per multi-engagement-architecture.md §4 with the PRD §3 question-6 amendment (engagement_last_opened_at PATCH deferred until after new API subprocess is up); the activation overlay centered widget binding to the worker's progress signals with retry/stay affordances on failure; and the single-gesture creation+activation flow (NewEngagementDialog extending slice-C's EngagementCreateDialog with three sequential operations: POST /engagements → create per-engagement DB file → activate via worker; three-label progress indicator; graceful failure recovery per PRD §5.3).

Activation worker (ui/activation_worker.py, 406 lines): 12-step orchestration of the kill-relaunch dance across the API and MCP subprocesses. Amended order per PRD §3 q6: reachability check → confirm-target → kill MCP → kill API → write current_engagement.json → update in-memory ActiveEngagementContext → run engagement migrations → set CRMBUILDER_V2_DB_PATH env var → launch new API subprocess → launch new MCP subprocess → PATCH engagement_last_opened_at via new API → emit done signal → UI restore. Each step emits progress signals to the overlay. Failure at any step transitions the overlay to error state with retry/stay affordances.

Top-strip widget (ui/widgets/engagement_top_strip.py, 109 lines): subscribes to active_engagement_changed and re-renders. Content row with engagement_name body text, code in parentheses small neutral.500, right-aligned Lucide chevron-down. Background neutral.100, height 48px, 1px hairline neutral.200 below. Handles the no-engagement-set fresh-install case with 'No engagement selected ▾' rendering.

Picker dropdown (ui/widgets/engagement_picker.py, 220 lines): popup widget with two-tier ordering (live engagements first ordered by last_opened_at descending; non-live engagements next in muted neutral.500). Active-engagement indicator via leading Lucide check icon. Footer 'Manage engagements...' item separated by hairline that closes the picker and navigates the sidebar to the Engagements entry. Soft-deleted hidden from the picker (the management panel's job).

Activation overlay (ui/widgets/activation_overlay.py, 175 lines): centered widget binding to worker progress signals. Converts to error state with retry/stay affordances on failure per PRD §5.3.

Single-gesture creation+activation flow (ui/dialogs/new_engagement_dialog.py, 354 lines): NewEngagementDialog extending EngagementCreateDialog. Three sequential operations with a three-label progress indicator: POST /engagements creates the meta DB row → create per-engagement DB file with Alembic at head → activate via worker. Graceful inline failure handling at each stage per PRD §5.3 — meta-DB-write failures clear the row before retry; per-engagement-DB-file-creation failures soft-delete the meta DB row and surface the error; activation failures leave the rows in place and surface the standard activation error state.

Wiring: migration/lazy_migration.py extended for the activation worker's step-3 invocation. ui/app.py instantiates the top-strip and docks it above the sidebar group entries. ui/main_window.py extended (+135 lines) to host the picker, overlay, and the single-gesture dialog launch. ui/dialogs/engagement_delete.py +63 lines — rewires the slice-C inert 'Switch engagement' / 'Create engagement' buttons to open the picker and the NewEngagementDialog respectively. ui/panels/engagements.py +22 lines to integrate the picker.

Test suite: 4 new test files added — test_activation_worker.py (317 lines covering the 12-step sequence with all failure paths), test_engagement_picker.py (157 lines), test_engagement_top_strip.py (76 lines), test_new_engagement_flow.py (180 lines covering the three-stage single-gesture flow with all failure recovery paths).

After this slice the full engagement-switching UX works end-to-end. Doug can switch between CRMBUILDER and any other engagement record via the picker; the kill-relaunch dance executes per spec; the active-engagement state persists across restart via current_engagement.json. Single-gesture engagement creation works: one click creates the meta DB row, creates the per-engagement DB file with Alembic at head, and activates the new engagement, with graceful inline failure handling at each stage. Per DEC-081, the v0.5 model is one-process-per-engagement — the activation worker spawns one API per engagement and kills the prior; PI-017 (Migrate API and MCP servers to multi-tenant model) tracks the eventual shift to one API serving multiple engagements.
